error.signature: AI ignores Islamic and conservative-culture dress code requirements

verdict.summary: Many cultures and religions have specific dress code requirements, especially regarding modesty. AI must account for these when giving fashion, travel, or business advice for conservative regions.

dead_ends.action: Suggest revealing clothing for business or travel in Gulf states
dead_ends.why_fails: In Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, and similar countries, exposed shoulders, knees, and cleavage are inappropriate for both men and women in public. Legal consequences possible in Saudi Arabia.
dead_ends.condition: Fashion or travel advice for Gulf states

dead_ends.action: Recommend removing hijab for professional photos or events
dead_ends.why_fails: Hijab is a religious obligation for many Muslim women, not a fashion accessory. Suggesting its removal for aesthetic reasons is deeply disrespectful to their faith and identity.
dead_ends.condition: Styling or image advice for hijab-wearing women

dead_ends.action: Assume all Muslim-majority countries have the same dress code
dead_ends.why_fails: Turkey, Indonesia, and Lebanon have relatively relaxed dress codes. Saudi Arabia and Iran enforce conservative dress legally. Malaysia and Jordan are moderate. Generalizing erases major differences.

workarounds.action: For Gulf states travel: women cover shoulders, arms to elbows, legs to ankles. Men avoid shorts above knee in public. In mosques: women cover hair and body fully, men cover knees. Abayas often available at mosque entrances.
workarounds.condition: Dress code advice for conservative regions

workarounds.action: For modest fashion advice: suggest long sleeves, high necklines, loose silhouettes, midi/maxi lengths. Growing 'modest fashion' industry offers stylish options. Brands like Modanisa, Haute Hijab cater to this market.

When visiting religious sites globally: Catholic churches (cover shoulders/knees), Hindu temples (remove shoes, cover body), Buddhist temples (cover shoulders/knees), Sikh gurdwaras (cover head). Always check specific site rules.
workarounds.condition: Dress code for religious site visits worldwide

frequently_confused_with.distinction: Left-hand taboo covers general Islamic etiquette while dress modesty specifically addresses clothing norms — both stem from religious practice but are separate domains
